title: Please bring Firefox to OpenHarmony / HarmonyOS to support users on OpenHarmony 6.0 and newer.
content:
Hello Firefox Team,

I am writing to formally request the development and release of an official Firefox browser version optimized for OpenHarmony / HarmonyOS.

I am currently using a new smartphone running OpenHarmony 6.0. As the ecosystem grows rapidly, there is a substantial and increasing base of users who rely on this operating system for their daily stage environment. Currently, the browser options available on this platform are quite limited.

Firefox is widely celebrated for its privacy-focused features, strong extension/add-on ecosystem, and its independent Gecko rendering engine. Having Firefox natively available on OpenHarmony would significantly enhance the browsing experience, privacy security, and freedom of choice for millions of users within this new ecosystem.

Could the development team please consider adding OpenHarmony support to the official roadmap? Alternatively, if there is an existing experimental build, a community port, or a workaround to compile it for this architecture, I would greatly appreciate any guidance.

Thank you for your time, dedication to the open web, and consideration of this request!

Best regards
